raw_title: Scanning vs. Thorough Processing the News: Antecedents of First- and Second-Level Incidental Exposure and the Role of the Relevance Appraisal
title_en: Scanning vs. Thorough Processing the News: Antecedents of First- and Second-Level Incidental Exposure and the Role of the Relevance Appraisal
title_zh: Scanning vs. Thorough Processing the News: Antecedents of First- and Second-Level Incidental Exposure and the Role of the Relevance Appraisal
plain_summary_en: This paper studies Scanning vs. Thorough Processing the News: Antecedents of First- and Second-Level Incidental Exposure and the Role of the Relevance Appraisal. In plain terms, it asks what is happening, why it matters for public communication, and what evidence the authors use. The likely focus is Relevance (law), Psychology, Public relations, Political science. The abstract suggests a mixed methods approach, which makes the work useful for understanding how communication changes in everyday life.

The likely focus is Relevance (law), Psychology, Public relations, Political science. research_direction_en: The paper points to an active research direction around Relevance (law), Psychology, Public relations, Political science, especially how institutions, platforms, and audiences shape communication outcomes.
